title: Analytics & CRO
excerpt: GA4, Mixpanel, server-side tagging, experimentation. The feedback loop that turns a site into a compounding asset.
lede: GA4, Mixpanel, server-side tagging, experimentation. The feedback loop that turns a site into a compounding asset. Most analytics setups we inherit are broken in at least three places.

deliverables.title: Analytics audit
deliverables.desc: GA4, GTM, pixel health. We find what's firing wrong, what's missing, what's lying to you.

deliverables.title: Server-side tracking
deliverables.desc: First-party data, cookieless measurement. Your numbers stay accurate post-iOS updates.

deliverables.title: Dashboard build
deliverables.desc: Looker Studio or Mixpanel. Revenue-focused, not vanity-metric-focused.

deliverables.title: CRO research
deliverables.desc: Heatmaps, session recordings, user interviews, funnel analysis. We find the friction before we guess at fixes.

deliverables.title: A/B testing
deliverables.desc: Hypothesis-driven tests with statistical significance requirements. No more HiPPO decisions.

deliverables.title: Experimentation roadmap
deliverables.desc: A rolling 90-day pipeline of tests, ranked by expected lift × confidence × effort.

process_title: From broken tracking to compounding wins.

process_steps.title: Audit
process_steps.desc: GA4 configuration, GTM container, pixel health, data layer. We find every gap.

process_steps.title: Fix & rebuild
process_steps.desc: Server-side setup, consent mode, clean data layer. Tracking you can trust.

process_steps.title: Dashboard
process_steps.desc: Revenue-first reporting. The right numbers in the right hands.

process_steps.title: CRO research
process_steps.desc: Qualitative + quantitative: heatmaps, sessions, interviews, funnel teardown.

process_steps.title: Test & iterate
process_steps.desc: First test live. Two-week test cycles. Results compound over quarters.

case_title: Clara & Co — 2.4× checkout conversion in 90 days.
case_desc: A DTC beauty brand tracking revenue with broken attribution. We rebuilt tracking server-side, identified a 3-step checkout drop-off, ran two tests, and doubled conversion before the next peak season.

faqs.q: Our GA4 is a mess. Can you fix it?
faqs.a: That's the most common thing we're asked. Yes — we do a full rebuild, not a patch job.

faqs.q: Do you handle consent and GDPR?
faqs.a: Yes. Consent mode v2, server-side first-party tracking, and geo-based consent logic baked in.

faqs.q: What testing tool do you use?
faqs.a: Depends on stack. VWO, Optimizely, or native Next.js feature flags for engineering-heavy clients.

faqs.q: How long before we see results?
faqs.a: Tracking fixes are immediate. CRO lifts compound over 3–6 months of testing. We show lift from test 1.

cta_bottom: Make your data tell the truth.

title: Brand & Strategy
excerpt: Positioning, naming, visual identity and messaging.
lede: Positioning, naming, visual identity and messaging. The foundation every other marketing dollar you spend will lean on.

deliverables.title: Brand audit
deliverables.desc: Where you stand today. What's working, what's noise, and what competitors are getting away with.

deliverables.title: Positioning
deliverables.desc: A one-sentence claim that your whole team can repeat and defend.

deliverables.title: Naming
deliverables.desc: Product names, campaign names, sub-brand architecture. Done properly.

deliverables.title: Visual identity
deliverables.desc: Logo, type, color, motion — a complete visual system, not a single mark.

deliverables.title: Voice & messaging
deliverables.desc: How you sound, on a billboard and in a chat reply.

deliverables.title: Brand guidelines
deliverables.desc: A practical playbook your team and vendors can actually follow.

process_title: A brand sprint, start to finish.

process_steps.title: Interviews & audit
process_steps.desc: Stakeholder interviews, customer calls, competitive mapping. We hand you the honest picture.

process_steps.title: Positioning workshop
process_steps.desc: A two-day off-site (or Zoom room). We leave with a positioning statement you'd bet the company on.

process_steps.title: Identity design
process_steps.desc: Two visual directions, one round of refinement. No design-by-committee.

process_steps.title: System build
process_steps.desc: Guidelines, components, templates. Everything your team needs to run without us.

process_steps.title: Launch support
process_steps.desc: Rollout plan, internal launch kit, and an optional first campaign to put the brand to work.

case_desc: We rebuilt Ummah Travel's positioning, identity and booking site end-to-end. Eight weeks to ship, and the results outlived the launch quarter.

faqs.q: How much does a brand sprint cost?
faqs.a: Most full brand sprints land between $35–85k, depending on scope (naming, sub-brands, motion, etc). We'll scope precisely after a discovery call.

faqs.q: Do you work with early-stage startups?
faqs.a: Yes — we have a "Lite" version of the brand sprint tailored for pre-Series A companies that still need a serious foundation.

faqs.q: Can you build the website after?
faqs.a: That's the whole point. The identity we design is the identity we ship to production, on the same engagement.

faqs.q: What if we already have a logo?
faqs.a: Totally fine. Plenty of our engagements start with positioning and messaging while keeping the existing mark.

title: Mobile Apps
excerpt: iOS, Android, and cross-platform apps. We design, build, ship — and then run the marketing.
lede: iOS, Android, and cross-platform apps. We design, build, ship — and then run the marketing that actually drives installs and retention.
deliverables_title: What we build, end to end.

deliverables.title: Product strategy
deliverables.desc: JTBD, feature prioritization, monetization model. What belongs in v1 and what waits.

deliverables.title: UX prototype
deliverables.desc: Clickable Figma prototype tested with 5+ real users before a line of code is written.

deliverables.title: Visual design
deliverables.desc: Native patterns, not web-in-a-webview. iOS and Android feel native on their own terms.

deliverables.title: App build
deliverables.desc: React Native or Swift/Kotlin depending on complexity. Fully typed, fully tested.

deliverables.title: Backend & APIs
deliverables.desc: Supabase, Firebase, or bespoke Node. Auth, push, payments, analytics wired in.

deliverables.title: Launch & ASO
deliverables.desc: App Store Optimization, store listings, screenshots, launch campaign. The install funnel matters too.

process_steps.title: Product definition
process_steps.desc: User research, feature spec, success metrics. We ship v1, not v4.

process_steps.title: Prototype & test
process_steps.desc: Figma prototype in testers' hands. We find the rough edges before code.

process_steps.title: Design & architecture
process_steps.desc: Native UI system, backend schema, API contracts. Engineering and design in lockstep.

process_steps.title: Sprint build
process_steps.desc: Two-week sprints with demoable builds in TestFlight / Play internal.

process_steps.title: Beta & launch
process_steps.desc: Closed beta, iteration, store submission. App Review is our problem, not yours.

case_title: Skynet — delivery app that saved itself $42/install.
case_desc: Rebuilt a performance-dragging hybrid app as a native React Native experience. Install-to-first-order conversion tripled, and acquisition cost dropped 42%.

faqs.q: React Native or native Swift/Kotlin?
faqs.a: Depends on your roadmap. 80% of apps we build are React Native — 20% need native-native because of performance-critical paths.

faqs.q: Do you handle the App Store submission?
faqs.a: Yes. We manage the full submission, review cycle, and any rejection appeals.

faqs.q: Can you integrate with our existing backend?
faqs.a: Absolutely. We've integrated with everything from Rails monoliths to serverless Supabase. Bring your APIs.

faqs.q: What about ongoing maintenance?
faqs.a: We offer month-to-month retainers for iOS/Android updates, bug fixes, and feature additions after launch.

cta_bottom: App in your head, shipping in 12 weeks.

title: Paid Media
excerpt: Meta, Google, TikTok, programmatic. We build paid media engines where creative, audience and landing pages work as one.
lede: Meta, Google, TikTok, programmatic, connected TV. We build paid media engines where the creative rotation, audience strategy and landing pages all work as one system — not five separate vendors blaming each other.

deliverables.title: Account audit
deliverables.desc: Historical account teardown. What's working, what's wasted, what to kill this week.

deliverables.title: Channel strategy
deliverables.desc: Which platforms deserve spend, why, and in what ratio. Based on your margins, not vendor kickbacks.

deliverables.title: Creative system
deliverables.desc: Ad variants in a modular system so we can rotate 50+ creatives without bottlenecking.

deliverables.title: Landing pages
deliverables.desc: Conversion pages built specifically for cold traffic. Shared design language with your brand.

deliverables.title: Measurement
deliverables.desc: Server-side tracking, UTM hygiene, ROAS dashboards your CFO will actually read.

deliverables.title: Weekly optimization
deliverables.desc: Bid adjustments, creative rotation, negative keywords. Compound wins.

process_steps.title: Audit & intake
process_steps.desc: Full account teardown. Budget allocation hypothesis.

process_steps.title: Strategy & build
process_steps.desc: Campaign structures, audience segments, creative brief. Tracking and pixels audited.

process_steps.title: Creative sprint
process_steps.desc: 15–30 ad variants shot/produced. Modular, testable, brand-safe.

process_steps.title: Launch & learn
process_steps.desc: Controlled spend, rapid iteration. The first four weeks are about finding winners.

process_steps.title: Scale
process_steps.desc: Winners scale. Losers die. New tests every two weeks.

case_title: Skynet — 42% lower CPA across $2.4M in spend.
case_desc: Took a Meta + Google account with 11 ad sets firing in circles. Consolidated, rebuilt the creative, rebuilt the funnel. CPA halved within 8 weeks.

faqs.q: What's your minimum media spend?
faqs.a: We take on accounts with $20k+/month in media spend. Below that, the management fee eats the delta.

faqs.q: Do you charge a % of spend?
faqs.a: Flat monthly fee, tied to scope. We don't want our revenue going up just because your spend went up.

faqs.q: Which platforms do you run?
faqs.a: Meta, Google (Search + YouTube + Demand Gen), TikTok, LinkedIn, programmatic via DV360, and CTV via Vibe. We recommend based on your audience, not our comfort zone.

faqs.q: Can you fix a broken account?
faqs.a: That's most of what we do. Most accounts we inherit are 20–40% wasted spend.

title: Production & Content
excerpt: Photo, video, motion, copy. The fuel line for everything you publish.
lede: Photo, video, motion, copy. The fuel line for everything you publish — ads, social, site, campaigns. In-house production means no briefing a third party, no lost-in-translation, no missed brand voice.

deliverables.title: Photography
deliverables.desc: Studio and on-location. Product, lifestyle, team, editorial. We direct and deliver.

deliverables.title: Video production
deliverables.desc: 30s to 10min. Brand films, testimonials, product demos, social reels.

deliverables.title: Motion & animation
deliverables.desc: Logo animations, UI explainers, ad cuts, social loops. Rigged for re-use.

deliverables.title: Copywriting
deliverables.desc: Landing page copy, email sequences, brand storytelling. One voice, everywhere.

deliverables.title: UGC & testimonials
deliverables.desc: Managed testimonial capture with real customers. Scripted enough to convert, raw enough to trust.

deliverables.title: Asset management
deliverables.desc: Organized, tagged, stored. Every asset findable by your team in seconds.

process_steps.title: Creative brief
process_steps.desc: Goals, audiences, platforms, tone, mood references. The brief is the work.

process_steps.title: Pre-production
process_steps.desc: Shot list, talent, locations, props, styling. We over-prepare so shoot day runs.

process_steps.title: Shoot day(s)
process_steps.desc: Efficient, directed, on-brief. Typically 60–100 usable assets per day.

process_steps.title: Post-production
process_steps.desc: Edit, color, sound, motion. Two rounds of revision.

process_steps.title: Delivery
process_steps.desc: Formatted for every platform. Organized in your asset library.

case_title: Northwind Provisions — 156% DTC lift from one shoot.
case_desc: Northwind was running paid ads with product-on-white stock photos. We shot one lifestyle day — 80 assets across 4 product lines — and rebuilt their ad creative. Revenue doubled in 90 days.

faqs.q: How much does a production day cost?
faqs.a: Studio days from $6k, on-location from $8k. Includes full crew, post-production, and final delivery.

faqs.q: Do you provide talent and locations?
faqs.a: Yes — we have a casting and location network. Or we can work with yours.

faqs.q: Can you produce for international markets?
faqs.a: We shoot in Pakistan, UAE and Canada, and travel globally for large productions.

faqs.q: Do we own the assets?
faqs.a: 100%. All raw files and finals transfer to you on completion.

cta_bottom: Got a brief? Let's make it.

title: SEO
excerpt: Technical SEO, editorial SEO, and link strategy as one engine.
lede: Technical SEO, editorial SEO, and link strategy as one engine. We treat search like an asset class — you invest, it compounds. We don't do shortcuts that break at the next algorithm update.

deliverables.title: Technical audit
deliverables.desc: Core Web Vitals, crawlability, index bloat, schema. 60+ checks, prioritized by impact.

deliverables.title: Keyword strategy
deliverables.desc: Clusters mapped to intent, mapped to funnel stage. The map before the content.

deliverables.title: Editorial calendar
deliverables.desc: Monthly themes, article briefs, internal linking plan. Writers have everything they need.

deliverables.title: On-page optimization
deliverables.desc: Titles, headers, schema, meta, image alts. Existing pages reoptimized for 2026 SERPs.

deliverables.title: Link strategy
deliverables.desc: Digital PR and earned links, not PBNs. Slower, but it survives.

deliverables.title: Reporting
deliverables.desc: Rankings, organic sessions, revenue attribution. Weekly Looker Studio dashboard.

process_steps.title: Audit & benchmark
process_steps.desc: Technical, content, backlink, competitor. You get a 40-page report and a prioritized Linear board.

process_steps.title: Quick wins
process_steps.desc: Fix index bloat, Core Web Vitals, obvious on-page gaps. Rankings typically move in weeks.

process_steps.title: Content sprint
process_steps.desc: First wave of new articles, optimized existing pages. Topic authority takes shape.

process_steps.title: Link acquisition
process_steps.desc: Digital PR campaigns, resource pages, contextual placements. Quality over quantity.

process_steps.title: Compound
process_steps.desc: Monthly content, technical hygiene, authority building. The asset grows quietly.

case_title: Ummah Travel — 4.2× organic traffic in 6 months.
case_desc: A pilgrimage-booking site competing against giants. We rebuilt site architecture around pilgrimage intent clusters, shipped 80 editorial pieces, and watched rankings climb against 10× bigger competitors.

faqs.q: How quickly will I rank?
faqs.a: Technical fixes can move rankings in days. Content competes in 3–6 months. Trust-building is a 12-month game. Anyone promising faster is selling a short squeeze.

faqs.q: Do you do link building?
faqs.a: Yes, but through digital PR and genuine relationships — not PBNs or sponsored posts. Slower, safer, durable.

faqs.q: Can you write the content?
faqs.a: We have in-house writers for most verticals, plus a vetted network for specialists (medical, legal, fintech).

faqs.q: How do you report?
faqs.a: Looker Studio dashboard updated daily. Monthly strategy call. We focus on revenue from organic, not just rankings.

title: Social & Content
excerpt: Calendars that actually get filled. Photo, video, motion and copy produced in-house.
lede: Calendars that actually get filled. Photo, video, motion and copy — produced in-house so it doesn't bottleneck. We own the content engine so you can run it without chasing freelancers.
deliverables_title: Every piece of the content engine.

deliverables.title: Content strategy
deliverables.desc: Audience personas, platform mix, content pillars. The why before the what.

deliverables.title: Editorial calendar
deliverables.desc: 60-day rolling calendar, scheduled and briefed. No more scrambling on Monday morning.

deliverables.title: Photo & video
deliverables.desc: Studio or on-location shoots. Social cuts, reels, stills — all from one production day.

deliverables.title: Copywriting
deliverables.desc: Captions, long-form, email. One voice, everywhere, consistently.

deliverables.title: Community management
deliverables.desc: Replies, DMs, comments — with a tone guide so it sounds like you, not a VA.

deliverables.title: Performance reporting
deliverables.desc: Monthly analytics: reach, saves, link clicks, follower quality. We cut what doesn't earn.

process_title: 60 days to a full content operation.

process_steps.title: Audit & strategy
process_steps.desc: Existing content teardown, competitor benchmarking, pillar definition. We find what's already working.

process_steps.title: First shoot
process_steps.desc: Brand content library built. 30–60 assets usable across all platforms.

process_steps.title: Calendar live
process_steps.desc: First 30 days of posts scheduled. Captions reviewed and approved.

process_steps.title: Cadence
process_steps.desc: Weekly publishing rhythm locked. Monthly shoots. Quarterly strategy reviews.

case_title: Parable Foods — from 0 to 48k in 6 months.
case_desc: A premium food brand launching into retail needed social proof before they had shelf space. We built the content engine from scratch — strategy, shoots, and community — and grew their audience faster than their distribution.

faqs.q: How many posts per month?
faqs.a: Depends on the plan — typically 12–20 on Instagram, 4–8 on LinkedIn. We prioritize quality over volume.

faqs.q: Do you manage the accounts or just create content?
faqs.a: Both options available. Most clients have us manage end-to-end including community management.

faqs.q: Do you film locally or can you travel?
faqs.a: We're based in Lahore, Dubai, and Toronto. We travel for shoots globally — travel costs added at cost.

faqs.q: What if we don't like a post?
faqs.a: Two rounds of feedback included on every piece. We build a tone guide so edits get rarer over time.

cta_bottom: Ready for a content engine that actually runs?

title: Websites & Platforms
excerpt: Marketing sites, e-commerce, and custom portals. Designed and built by the same team.
lede: Marketing sites, e-commerce storefronts, and custom portals. Designed and built by the same team — which is why Digibit sites consistently score 95+ on Lighthouse and ship in weeks, not quarters.

deliverables.title: Discovery & IA
deliverables.desc: Sitemap, user flows, and content model. We don't start wireframing until this is signed off.

deliverables.title: UX wireframes
deliverables.desc: Grayscale, mobile-first. Interactive Figma prototype for usability testing.

deliverables.title: Visual design
deliverables.desc: Every page, every state. Dark mode, empty states, error states — properly designed.

deliverables.title: Frontend build
deliverables.desc: Next.js, React, or Webflow depending on fit. Typed, accessible, fast.

deliverables.title: CMS & integrations
deliverables.desc: Sanity, Contentful, Shopify. Your marketing team can publish without a ticket.

deliverables.title: Launch & QA
deliverables.desc: Cross-browser, cross-device, cross-accessibility. We don't hand off broken things.

process_title: Six weeks from kickoff to launch.

process_steps.title: Discovery
process_steps.desc: Stakeholder interviews, analytics dive, competitor teardown. We find what's actually wrong before we propose a fix.

process_steps.title: Information architecture
process_steps.desc: Sitemap and content model locked. No surprise sections appear during build.

process_steps.title: Design system
process_steps.desc: Type scale, grid, components, page templates. The whole visual language in one place.

process_steps.title: Build
process_steps.desc: Component-driven development in a staging environment. You review daily.

process_steps.title: Launch
process_steps.desc: QA, performance tuning, SEO technicals, DNS cutover. 48-hour hypercare window after go-live.

case_title: Ummah Travel — the booking site that actually converts.
case_desc: Rebuilt from a legacy stack to a headless Next.js + Sanity setup. Page weight dropped 72%, organic conversion rate more than doubled.

faqs.q: How much does a site cost?
faqs.a: Marketing sites typically $40–120k. E-commerce and custom platforms $80–250k+. We scope precisely after a discovery call.

faqs.q: Webflow, Next.js, or something else?
faqs.a: We pick the stack that fits your team. If your marketers will edit weekly, Webflow or Sanity. If it's app-like, Next.js with a headless CMS.

faqs.q: Do you host it too?
faqs.a: We can, or we hand off to Vercel / Netlify / your cloud. Most clients choose Vercel because it's frictionless.

faqs.q: Will it be accessible?
faqs.a: WCAG 2.2 AA is our baseline, not an add-on. We test with screen readers and keyboard nav before shipping.

cta_bottom: Ready for a site that ships?
